response.justification: Seasoned generalist with 15+ years of full-stack engineering and strong teaching/mentoring credentials, plus recent exposure to MCP and agentic workflows at re:cinq. However, the resume shows only about a year of dedicated GenAI work — well short of the 8+ years required — and lacks explicit Python, vector database, or RAG experience. Could be a fit if the team weighs systems breadth and customer-facing skills over deep GenAI tenure, otherwise this is below bar for a Staff GenAI FDE.
strengths: 15+ years shipping production systems across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backends
Current hands-on work with MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks at re:cinq
Demonstrated technical-leadership and stakeholder communication via workshops, courses, and conference talks
concerns: Python is not listed in skills; AI/GenAI tenure is roughly 1 year, far below the 8+ year requirement
No mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, or LLM evaluation/observability pipelines
BS from MIT (2009–2012) overlaps oddly with concurrent full-time roles in Europe, warranting verification

key_factors.factor: Missing RAG / vector DB / eval-pipeline experience
Recent MCP and agentic workflow contributions
